title: The Making of Star Wars
content:
{{Top|rwm|italics=1}}
{{Youmay|the documentary|the [[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film|book]]}}

|image=[[File:The_Making_of_Star_Wars_1977_doc_title_card.jpg]]
|title=''The Making of Star Wars''
|format=
|production=

Oh, and an entertainment journalist.|author=Jenson, Jeff|date=2012-02-09|archivedate=20120211140857}}</ref><ref name="YBY">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref>
|published in=''[[Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]''

{{Quote|When I saw those films, what I realized was that that could be a potential career. And then the following year after ''Star Wars'', I saw ''The Making of Star Wars'', and that completely transformed me. That's when I saw people behind the scenes, doing the craft, doing the artwork, doing the model building. I knew. I said, 'That's what I want to do.' That was exactly what I wanted to do.|[[Doug Chiang]]|<ref name="Interview">{{SW|url=news/doug-chiang-interview|text="There is a Strength in Asian Culture": A Conversation with Lucasfilm Legend Doug Chiang|author=Jenn Fujikawa|date=2021-05-19}}</ref>}}
'''''The Making of Star Wars''''' is an hour-long television special (49 minutes without commercials), produced and directed by [[Robert Guenette]], which originally aired on the [[ABC Television Network]] on [[September 16]], [[1977]]. ''Star Wars'' producer [[Gary Kurtz]] acted as Executive Producer for the special.

The special features interviews with the writers, producers, and cast members of the film then known simply as [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']], including Kurtz, [[George Lucas]], [[Mark Hamill]], [[Harrison Ford]], [[Carrie Fisher]], and [[Alec Guinness]]; and is hosted by [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] (voiced by [[Anthony Daniels]]) and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] (performed by [[Kenny Baker]]), with voiceover narration by [[William Conrad]]. The show was produced by [[20th Century Studios|20th Century Fox]] Television in association with the Star Wars Corporation (one of the early subsidiaries of [[Lucasfilm]]).

In addition to ''Star Wars'' and Lucas's previous films [[Wikipedia:THX 1138|''THX 1138'']] and [[Wikipedia:American Graffiti|''American Graffiti'']], the special also includes footage from [[Wikipedia:The Black Swan (film)|''The Black Swan'']], [[Wikipedia:The Mark of Zorro (1940 film)|''The Mark of Zorro'']], [[Wikipedia:My Darling Clementine|''My Darling Clementine'']], the [[Wikipedia:Flash Gordon Conquers the Universe|''Flash Gordon Conquers the Universe'']] episode "Doom of the Dictator," the [[Wikipedia:Flash Gordon's Trip to Mars|''Flash Gordon's Trip to Mars'']] episode "The Miracle of Magic," [[Wikipedia:Twelve O'Clock High|''Twelve O'Clock High'']], and the Atari game [[Wikipedia:Anti-Aircraft (video game)|''Anti-Aircraft'']], which Fisher is seen playing during her interview.

==Home video release {{SectionFlag}}==
[[File:The_Making_of_Star_Wars_1977_doc_VHS.jpg|thumb|250px|VHS cover]]
The special was the first ''Star Wars'' material to be released to the home video market in [[1979]] by [[Wikipedia:Magnetic Video|Magnetic Video]]. It was reissued on video in [[1980]] with a trailer for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which had been released that year. This trailer was not featured on the [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|DVD box set]] issued in [[2004]].

In [[1982]], it was reissued to video by 20th Century Fox on [[Wikipedia:VHS|VHS]], [[Wikipedia:Betamax|Betamax]], [[Wikipedia:CED|CED]], and [[Wikipedia:Laserdisc|Laserdisc]] as part of a double feature with the 1980 special ''[[SPFX: The Empire Strikes Back]]''. It was reissued separately in Japan on Laserdisc in 1992, and was reissued as a triple feature with ''SPFX'' and the [[1983]] special ''[[Classic Creatures: Return of the Jedi]]''. It was also included in the [[Star Wars: The Complete Saga|''Star Wars'' saga Blu-ray]] box set released in [[2011]].<ref name="TFNSWBD">{{WebCite|url=http://theforce.net/latestnews/story/MTFBWY_SW_BluRay_Details_Released_137998.asp|text=MTFBWY: ''SW'' Blu-Ray Details Released|author=Geller, Eric|date=2011-05-04|archivedate=20190730154757}}</ref>

==Alternate version {{SectionFlag}}==
[[File:Clarion-Ledger_Jackson_MS_1977-09-16.jpg|thumb|250px|Aired immediately following the George Burns episode of ''[[The Muppet Show]]'']]
In [[1995]], this special was released on VHS as a mail-in offer with the [[Wikipedia:Kellogg Company|Kellogg Company]] to tie in with the last video and Laserdisc releases of the original versions of the [[original trilogy]].<ref name="SWArchive-25 Years of Star Wars and Kellogg">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iii/release/promo/news20050615.html|text=25 Years of ''Star Wars'' and Kellogg}}</ref> This version replaces William Conrad's voiceover with that of [[Don LaFontaine]], but is otherwise unmodified. This version has not been issued on [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] nor any other format.

Portions of the special were included in [[Star Wars: Connections|''The Story of Star Wars'']], a [[Wikipedia:VideoNow|VideoNow]] release that was also released on DVD as a promotional bonus at [[Wikipedia:Wal-Mart|Wal-Mart]] stores alongside the DVD release of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']].
